2605.07776.title: Tracing Uncertainty in Language Model "Reasoning"
2605.07776.paper_summary: Language model (LM) "reasoning", commonly described as Chain-of-Thought or test-time scaling, often improves benchmark performance, but the dynamics underlying this process remain poorly understood. We study these dynamics through the lens of uncertainty quantification by treating the "reasoning" traces, the intermediate token sequences generated by LMs, as evolving model states. We summarize each trace by an uncertainty trace profile: a small set of features describing the shape of the uncertainty signal over its trace, such as its slope and linearity. We find that across five LMs evaluated on GSM8K and ProntoQA, these profiles predict whether a trace yields a correct final answer with AUROC up to 0.807, improving markedly on recent related work. We reach AUROC 0.801 using only the first few hundred tokens of full traces, suggesting that errors can be detected early in the generation. A detailed comparison of correct and incorrect traces further reveals qualitatively distinct uncertainty profiles, with correct traces showing a steeper and less linear decline in uncertainty. Together, the results suggest that our method, grounded in decision-making under uncertainty, provides a principled lens for studying the generative process underlying LM "reasoning".
2605.07776.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型 (LM) 的「推理」，通常被描述為思維鏈或測試時擴展，常常能提高基準性能，但這一過程背後的動態仍然不甚了解。<lb/>我們通過不確定性量化的視角來研究這些動態，將「推理」痕跡，即由 LMs 生成的中間標記序列，視為演變中的模型狀態。<lb/>我們用不確定性痕跡輪廓來總結每個痕跡：一組小特徵描述不確定性信號在其痕跡上的形狀，例如其斜率和線性。<lb/>我們發現，在對 GSM8K 和 ProntoQA 評估的五個 LMs 中，這些輪廓預測痕跡是否產生正確的最終答案，AUROC 高達 0.807，顯著改善了近期相關工作。<lb/>我們僅使用完整痕跡的前幾百個標記就達到了 AUROC 0.801，這表明錯誤可以在生成的早期被檢測到。<lb/>對正確和不正確痕跡的詳細比較進一步揭示了質量上不同的不確定性輪廓，正確痕跡顯示出不確定性下降的斜率更陡且不那麼線性。<lb/>綜合來看，這些結果表明，我們的方法以不確定性下的決策為基礎，為研究 LM 「推理」背後的生成過程提供了一個原則性的視角。
2605.07776.author: Nils Grünefeld et.al.
2605.07776.authors: Nils Grünefeld, Bertram Højer, Philipp Mondorf, Barbara Plank, Anna Rogers, Christian Hardmeier, Stefan Heinrich, Jes Frellsen
